decisions.title: Contract packaging

options.name: Single main contractor package
Split groundworks and main build packages

decisions.notes: Project brief currently prefers a single main contract, but an early groundworks split remains possible.

decisions.title: Courtyard drainage build-up

options.name: Standard slot drains and falls
Concealed drainage channels with extra sump capacity

decisions.notes: Courtyard detailing will affect groundworks coordination and threshold levels.

decisions.title: Steel frame finish

options.name: Standard intumescent coating where required
Higher-finish exposed steel package

decisions.notes: Depends on whether any steel remains visible internally.

decisions.title: Roof covering strategy

options.name: Cost-led pitched covering with standard warm roof links
Upgrade to premium standing seam / enhanced link detailing

decisions.notes: Warm roof link detailing is fixed; final finish level is still open.

decisions.title: Cladding species

options.name: Larch (treated, hidden fixings)
Cedar (treated, hidden fixings)

decisions.notes: Courtyard treated as envelope for reporting; courtyard finishing tracked separately long-term.

decisions.title: Wall build-up / insulation thickness

options.name: Baseline compliant fabric build-up
Enhanced insulation and airtightness package

decisions.notes: Trade-off between upfront cost and operational performance.

decisions.title: Large slider specification

options.name: Standard sliding system
Higher-performance slimline slider system

decisions.notes: Applies to the two principal large openings in the living volume.

decisions.title: Solar control glazing

options.name: Standard double glazing specification
Targeted solar-control glass to south/west elevations

decisions.notes: Useful if overheating risk remains after passive shading review.

decisions.title: MVHR confirmation

options.name: Proceed with MVHR throughout
Natural ventilation fallback with local extract only

decisions.notes: Project assumptions currently treat MVHR as likely.

decisions.title: Electrical allowance level

options.name: Standard switch/socket and lighting package
Enhanced lighting scenes and joinery-integrated power

decisions.notes: Needs coordination with kitchen, joinery, and external lighting decisions.

decisions.title: Kitchen cabinetry and worktops

options.name: Vermland cabinetry and worktops (£24,000)
Higher-spec fully bespoke cabinetry and worktops

decisions.notes: Current preferred supplier is Vermland at £24,000 ex VAT for cabinetry and worktops.

decisions.title: Appliance package

options.name: Allowance of £15,000 for appliances
Premium appliance package above allowance

decisions.notes: Current working assumption is a £15,000 appliance spend.

decisions.title: Bath selection (1 bath)

options.name: Standard acrylic bath
Stone resin / premium bath

decisions.notes: Single bath item for the family bathroom.

decisions.title: Shower package (3 showers)

options.name: Standard concealed thermostatic sets x3
Premium brassware / larger heads x3

decisions.notes: Applies across three shower locations.

decisions.title: Basin / sink selection (4 sinks)

options.name: Standard ceramic basins x4
Designer basin package x4

decisions.notes: Includes family bathroom, ensuites, and powder room sinks.

decisions.title: Bathroom wall finish

options.name: Targeted tiling to wet areas only
More extensive full-height tiling package

decisions.notes: This one overlaps with finishes and should be coordinated before procurement.

decisions.title: Internal door specification

options.name: Paint-grade flush doors
Higher-spec veneer / rebated detail doors

decisions.notes: Impacts second-fix joinery package and decorating scope.

decisions.title: Wardrobe extent

options.name: Essential fitted wardrobes only
Expanded fitted storage package

decisions.notes: Can be postponed or phased if contingency tightens.

decisions.title: Floor finish level

options.name: Polished terrazzo to c.100 sqm + engineered oak to 110 sqm + basic garage concrete finish
Higher-spec terrazzo and premium wide-board oak upgrade

decisions.notes: Current working assumption is polished terrazzo to the main living zones, engineered oak to the remaining house areas, and a basic non-polished garage concrete finish.

decisions.title: Decorating scope

options.name: Standard paint system
Enhanced finish schedule with feature areas

decisions.notes: Can be held until later once rooms are fully coordinated.

decisions.title: EV charging allowance

options.name: Single standard charger
Load-managed charger with future PV/battery integration

decisions.notes: Useful to coordinate with phase 2 PV and battery ambitions.

decisions.title: Site welfare and storage setup

options.name: Baseline welfare cabin and compact secure storage
Expanded welfare, drying room, and larger secure storage

decisions.notes: Upgrade above the current prelims allowance if site occupation becomes more demanding.

decisions.title: Temporary access and hardstanding strategy

options.name: Baseline stone access track and simple contractor hardstanding
Extended temporary hardstanding and reusable trackway system

decisions.notes: Only needed if ground conditions or vehicle frequency require a more robust temporary setup.

decisions.title: Below-ground service entry coordination

options.name: Baseline consolidated service entries and standard sleeves
Enhanced future-proofing with additional sleeves and segregated entries

decisions.notes: Affects later M&E flexibility rather than the initial buildability of the slab.

decisions.title: Rainwater attenuation and chamber strategy

options.name: Baseline attenuation and standard chamber layout
Oversized attenuation with extra access chambers and maintenance points

decisions.notes: Relevant if drainage coordination tightens around the courtyard and glazing thresholds.

decisions.title: Secondary timber infill strategy

options.name: Baseline timber infill and conventional secondary framing
Upgraded pre-insulated or faster-install secondary framing package

decisions.notes: Potential time/certainty upgrade rather than a design-led upgrade.

decisions.title: Rainwater goods visibility

options.name: Baseline exposed PPC rainwater goods
Concealed rainwater goods and cleaner roof-edge detailing

decisions.notes: A visual upgrade that needs early coordination with roof and cladding interfaces.

decisions.title: Flat link roof build-up refinement

options.name: Baseline warm roof build-up
Higher-performance tapered insulation and refined edge build-up

decisions.notes: Useful if the final roof edges and parapet heights need tightening.

decisions.title: External soffit and reveal finish

options.name: Baseline painted board / standard lining to soffits and reveals
Timber-lined soffits and upgraded reveal detailing

decisions.notes: This is a visual uplift above the current envelope allowance.

decisions.title: Plinth and low-level wall finish

options.name: Baseline robust plain plinth finish
Refined masonry/render plinth detail

decisions.notes: Affects the perceived quality of the base detail around the house and garage.

decisions.title: Courtyard wall lining and detail package

options.name: Baseline cladding continuation and straightforward courtyard interfaces
Enhanced courtyard lining, trims, and refined junction detailing

decisions.notes: This is the main envelope-area upgrade that would sharpen the courtyard finish without touching long-term paving budgets.

decisions.title: Entrance door set

options.name: Baseline aluminium entrance door
Feature entrance door with higher-spec ironmongery and detailing

decisions.notes: Upgrade above the current glazing allowance if the entrance becomes a more expressive feature.

decisions.title: Garage door type

options.name: Baseline insulated sectional garage door
Higher-finish concealed or upgraded garage door

decisions.notes: Included here because it sits within the external openings package.

decisions.title: Glazing ironmongery and threshold detail

options.name: Baseline handles, thresholds, and standard glazing interfaces
Upgraded hardware and refined threshold/interface package

decisions.notes: A small but noticeable finish-led upgrade across the openings package.

decisions.title: Heating controls and zoning

options.name: Baseline zoning and standard room controls
Enhanced smart zoning and control package

decisions.notes: Useful to decide alongside the final room-by-room heating and lighting expectations.

decisions.title: Hot water cylinder and plant fit-out

options.name: Baseline cylinder and straightforward plant-room fit-out
Space-optimised and acoustically improved plant-room package

decisions.notes: This is a coordination and usability upgrade rather than a must-have.

decisions.title: External lighting package

options.name: Baseline task and safety external lighting
Architectural exterior lighting package

decisions.notes: This is above the baseline electrical allowance currently carried in the budget.

decisions.title: Data, Wi-Fi, and AV backbone

options.name: Baseline essential data points and standard Wi-Fi setup
Enhanced whole-house data, Wi-Fi, and AV-prep package

decisions.notes: Worth locking down before first-fix if you want cleaner infrastructure later.

decisions.title: Partition acoustic build-up

options.name: Baseline acoustic build-up
Enhanced acoustic partitions to key rooms

decisions.notes: Primarily affects bedroom privacy and the study/snug acoustic separation.

decisions.title: Stair substrate and construction approach

options.name: Baseline paint-grade stair substrate
Bespoke stair structure and upgraded substrate allowance

decisions.notes: The finish decision for balustrade and final appearance sits later in joinery, but this captures the structural allowance uplift.

decisions.title: Kitchen island finish and detailing

options.name: Baseline island finish within current Vermland allowance
Upgraded island finish, detailing, and end-panel treatment

decisions.notes: This sits above the current cabinetry baseline rather than replacing the main kitchen supplier decision.

decisions.title: Kitchen splashback

options.name: Baseline simple splashback treatment
Stone or slab splashback upgrade

decisions.notes: Kept separate from worktops because this often moves independently.

decisions.title: Kitchen sink and tap

options.name: Baseline undermount sink and standard mixer
Upgraded sink, filtered water, and higher-spec tap package

decisions.notes: This is deliberately broken out so you can decide it independently of the cabinetry supplier.

decisions.title: Utility cabinetry

options.name: Baseline essential utility storage
Full-height utility cabinetry and concealed storage

decisions.notes: This is a separate upgrade above the current combined kitchen/utility budget allowance.

decisions.title: Utility sink and tap

options.name: Baseline utility sink and tap
Upgraded utility sink, tap, and accessories

decisions.notes: Useful to separate from the main kitchen sink decision if the utility is more workhorse-oriented.

decisions.title: Pantry shelving and storage fit-out

options.name: Baseline simple shelving fit-out
Bespoke pantry shelving and internal storage package

decisions.notes: Only relevant if the pantry remains part of the final room arrangement.

decisions.title: Family bathroom WC

options.name: Baseline WC within current bathroom allowance
Upgraded wall-hung WC

decisions.notes: Local upgrade above the current bathroom package allowance.

decisions.title: Family bathroom basin

options.name: Baseline basin within current bathroom allowance
Upgraded designer basin

decisions.notes: This refines the family bathroom selection beyond the broader basin package decision.

decisions.title: Family bathroom shower fittings

options.name: Baseline concealed thermostatic shower set
Upgraded shower valve, head, and rail package

decisions.notes: One of the three shower decisions, separated by room so you can mix priorities.

decisions.title: Master ensuite WC

options.name: Baseline WC within current bathroom allowance
Upgraded wall-hung WC

decisions.notes: Use this if the principal ensuite is carrying a slightly higher spec than the other bathrooms.

decisions.title: Master ensuite basin

options.name: Baseline basin within current bathroom allowance
Upgraded basin and vanity detail

decisions.notes: This is the most likely basin upgrade if one bathroom is going to step above the others.

decisions.title: Master ensuite shower fittings

options.name: Baseline concealed thermostatic shower set
Upgraded shower valve, head, and handset package

decisions.notes: Separate from the family bathroom and ensuite 2 so the principal room can be prioritised if needed.

decisions.title: Ensuite 2 WC

options.name: Baseline WC within current bathroom allowance
Upgraded wall-hung WC

decisions.notes: Keeps the second ensuite independent from the master ensuite specification.

decisions.title: Ensuite 2 basin

options.name: Baseline basin within current bathroom allowance
Upgraded basin and vanity detail

decisions.notes: A smaller upgrade option if ensuite 2 needs more finish quality than the baseline.

decisions.title: Ensuite 2 shower fittings

options.name: Baseline concealed thermostatic shower set
Upgraded shower valve, head, and handset package

decisions.notes: Third of the shower decisions, split out so you can cost each room honestly.

decisions.title: Powder room WC

options.name: Baseline compact WC within current allowance
Upgraded wall-hung compact WC

decisions.notes: Small change individually, but it affects how polished the powder room feels.

decisions.title: Powder room basin

options.name: Baseline compact basin within current allowance
Upgraded statement basin and tap package

decisions.notes: This is often where a small spec uplift has a disproportionate visual return.

decisions.title: Master ensuite wall finish

options.name: Baseline wet-area tiling only
Full-height premium tile or stone wall finish

decisions.notes: A finish-led upgrade above the current bathroom wall allowance.

decisions.title: Ensuite 2 wall finish

options.name: Baseline wet-area tiling only
Full-height upgraded tile package

decisions.notes: Independent from the master ensuite so the rooms can diverge if needed.

decisions.title: Powder room wall finish

options.name: Baseline paint and local splashback only
Decorative full-wall finish package

decisions.notes: Useful if the powder room is being treated as a more expressive small room.

decisions.title: Principal bedroom wardrobes

options.name: Baseline fitted wardrobes within current allowance
Enhanced internal fit-out and wardrobe package

decisions.notes: Most likely place to push wardrobe quality if not all bedrooms get the same spec.

decisions.title: Bedroom 2 wardrobes

options.name: Baseline freestanding storage / no bespoke wardrobes
Fitted wardrobe package

decisions.notes: Allows bedroom 2 to stay leaner if the budget needs holding.

decisions.title: Bedroom 3 wardrobes

options.name: Baseline freestanding storage / no bespoke wardrobes
Fitted wardrobe package

decisions.notes: Mirrors bedroom 2 so both secondary bedrooms can be treated consistently or differently.

decisions.title: Stair balustrade and handrail finish

options.name: Baseline paint-grade balustrade and handrail
Bespoke timber/metal balustrade upgrade

decisions.notes: Separate from the stair structure decision so finish can be judged later.

decisions.title: Media or living-room joinery

options.name: No bespoke media joinery in current allowance
Integrated media and storage joinery package

decisions.notes: Explicitly separated so it does not quietly creep into the joinery budget later.

decisions.title: Utility and boot-room bench joinery

options.name: Baseline open shelving only
Bench, coat storage, and closed utility joinery package

decisions.notes: This is optional convenience joinery above the current baseline.

decisions.title: Security, alarm, and entry package

options.name: Baseline alarm and doorbell provision only
Enhanced alarm, access control, and CCTV-ready package

decisions.notes: Captured here rather than hidden in electrical extras later.

decisions.title: Water softener and filtration

options.name: No additional treatment beyond baseline plumbing scope
Whole-house water softener and filtration package

decisions.notes: Useful to decide now if plant-room and kitchen provisions are needed.

decisions.title: PV and battery future-proofing

options.name: Baseline passive allowance only
Additional conduits, plant space, and cabling for phase-2 PV/battery

decisions.notes: This does not install PV or batteries, it just reduces friction for the later phase.

decisions.title: Entrance hall floor finish

options.name: Baseline terrazzo allowance
Premium terrazzo pattern or inlay upgrade

decisions.notes: This is room-specific uplift above the whole-house flooring baseline.

decisions.title: Kitchen floor finish

options.name: Baseline terrazzo allowance
Premium terrazzo or stone floor upgrade

decisions.notes: Kept separate from living/dining so the kitchen can be weighted differently if needed.

decisions.title: Pantry and utility floor finish

options.name: Baseline hardwearing tile / terrazzo continuation
Upgraded tile finish

decisions.notes: These back-of-house areas do not need to match the public rooms exactly.

decisions.title: Living and dining floor finish

options.name: Baseline terrazzo allowance
Premium terrazzo detail upgrade

decisions.notes: Most visible floor area, so this is where a terrazzo uplift is likely to land if any does.

decisions.title: Study or snug floor finish

options.name: Baseline engineered oak allowance
Upgraded oak board and finish package

decisions.notes: Broken out separately because this room can read differently from the bedrooms.

decisions.title: Principal bedroom floor finish

options.name: Baseline engineered oak allowance
Upgraded oak board and finish package

decisions.notes: Lets the principal bedroom be treated above the other bedrooms if desired.

decisions.title: Bedroom 2 floor finish

options.name: Baseline engineered oak allowance
Upgraded oak board and finish package

decisions.notes: Keep as baseline unless the bedroom spec is being pushed upward deliberately.

decisions.title: Bedroom 3 floor finish

options.name: Baseline engineered oak allowance
Upgraded oak board and finish package

decisions.notes: Mirrors bedroom 2 so the secondary bedrooms can be compared like for like.

decisions.title: Landing and circulation floor finish

options.name: Baseline engineered oak allowance
Upgraded oak board and finish package

decisions.notes: Useful if the circulation spaces need a calmer or more durable spec than the bedrooms.

decisions.title: Family bathroom floor finish

options.name: Baseline porcelain tile allowance
Large-format tile or stone floor upgrade

decisions.notes: A finish-led upgrade above the baseline bathroom floor allowance.

decisions.title: Master ensuite floor finish

options.name: Baseline porcelain tile allowance
Large-format tile or stone floor upgrade

decisions.notes: This is the room most likely to get the higher-spec floor if one bathroom is prioritised.

decisions.title: Ensuite 2 floor finish

options.name: Baseline porcelain tile allowance
Large-format tile upgrade

decisions.notes: Matches the family bathroom logic but for the second ensuite.

decisions.title: Powder room floor finish

options.name: Baseline porcelain tile allowance
Decorative tile or stone floor upgrade

decisions.notes: A small uplift, but a room where it can read strongly.

decisions.title: Garage floor finish

options.name: Baseline power-floated concrete allowance
Sealed or resin-coated garage floor upgrade

decisions.notes: This sits above the current minimal garage finish assumption.

decisions.title: Entrance hall wall finish

options.name: Baseline paint finish
Specialist paint or limewash upgrade

decisions.notes: A modest upgrade that materially changes first impression quality.

decisions.title: Kitchen wall finish

options.name: Baseline paint plus standard splashback zones
Specialist paint or microcement feature finish

decisions.notes: This is above the splashback decision and captures wider wall finish upgrades.

decisions.title: Living and dining wall finish

options.name: Baseline paint finish
Specialist paint or feature-wall finish package

decisions.notes: Potentially one of the largest visible wall-finish upgrades in the project.

decisions.title: Study or snug wall finish

options.name: Baseline paint finish
Feature wall finish or specialist paint package

decisions.notes: Useful if this room is taking on a more characterful or darker finish palette.

decisions.title: Principal bedroom wall finish

options.name: Baseline paint finish
Specialist paint or feature wall finish

decisions.notes: Lets the principal bedroom separate from the other bedrooms if that matters to you.

decisions.title: Bedroom 2 wall finish

options.name: Baseline paint finish
Feature wall finish

decisions.notes: Held low because this is likely to stay close to baseline unless the design brief changes.

decisions.title: Bedroom 3 wall finish

options.name: Baseline paint finish
Feature wall finish

decisions.notes: Mirrors bedroom 2 to keep comparisons straightforward.

decisions.title: Utility wall finish

options.name: Baseline paint finish
Washable high-durability finish upgrade

decisions.notes: This is a practical rather than aesthetic upgrade.

decisions.title: Garage wall finish

options.name: Baseline taped and plastered utility finish
Painted upgraded garage wall finish

decisions.notes: This pushes the garage beyond the current minimal assumption.

decisions.title: Courtyard paving strategy

options.name: No current fit-out beyond long-term placeholder
Starter paving package if courtyard works advance into this phase

decisions.notes: This remains outside the active build baseline unless you consciously bring it forward.

decisions.title: Courtyard benching and joinery

options.name: No current courtyard joinery allowance
Built-in benching and courtyard joinery package

decisions.notes: Separate from paving so the courtyard can be phased more intentionally.
